caption: The video displays a screen recording of a webpage from the IRS website. The IRS logo is visible in the top left corner, and the navigation menu includes "SEARCH | HELP | MENU". The page contains detailed text regarding Required Minimum Distributions (RMDs). The content begins with information on when RMDs are due, stating, "the first RMD is due by April 1 of the year the they reach age 72, or the participant is no longer employed (if allowed by the plan). A 5% owner of the employer must begin taking RMDs at age 72." Below this, a section explains that "RMDs may not be rolled over to another IRA or retirement plan" and mentions the "RMD Comparison Chart." A bold header reads "RMD Calculations and 50% tax on missed distributions," followed by guidance for IRS trustees and plan administrators. Across the frames, a mouse cursor moves across the text, highlighting different words and phrases.
